examples.title: Pseudocode: Copy-on-Write
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CopyOnWrite
INPUT: pageTable, virtualAddress, newValue
OUTPUT: updated pageTable

BEGIN
 IF pageTable[virtualAddress] IS marked as COW THEN
 // Allocate new physical frame
 newFrame = AllocateNewFrame()
 // Copy contents from original frame to new frame
 CopyContents(pageTable[virtualAddress], newFrame)
 // Update page table to point to new frame
 pageTable[virtualAddress] = newFrame
 // Set new frame as read-write
 SetReadWrite(newFrame)
 END IF
 // Write new value to page
 WriteValue(newFrame, newValue)
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the Copy-on-Write mechanism. When a write operation is attempted on a page marked as COW, a new physical frame is allocated, the contents are copied from the original frame to the new frame, and the page table is updated to point to the new frame.

examples.title: Python: Copy-on-Write Simulation
examples.description: Simulates the Copy-on-Write mechanism in Python
examples.explanation: This Python code simulates the Copy-on-Write mechanism. It uses a PageTable class to manage pages and simulates the allocation of new physical frames, copying of contents, and updating of the page table.

examples.title: C: Copy-on-Write Implementation
examples.description: Implements the Copy-on-Write mechanism in C
examples.explanation: This C code implements the Copy-on-Write mechanism. It uses a page table to manage pages and simulates the allocation of new physical frames, copying of contents, and updating of the page table.
